creator: bakhtin@amt.ru
text:
Kernel panic 'memory address not aligned' if some pptp traffic goes through mpd/netgraph interface.

Dec 21 15:11:37 amtkit-ey su: bakhtin to root on /dev/ttyp0
panic: trap: memory address not aligned
cpuid = 0
KDB: enter: panic
[thread pid 14 tid 100002 ]
Stopped at      kdb_enter+0x68: ta              %xcc, 1
db> bt
Tracing pid 14 tid 100002 td 0xfffff800010816c0
panic() at panic+0x204
trap() at trap+0x4cc
-- memory address not aligned sfar=0xfffff800012178de sfsr=0x4002d %o7=0xc0391e3c --
rip_output() at rip_output+0x1a4
rip_send() at rip_send+0x4c
sosend_generic() at sosend_generic+0x5e4
sosend() at sosend+0x2c
ng_ksocket_rcvdata() at ng_ksocket_rcvdata+0xb8
ng_apply_item() at ng_apply_item+0x1b8
ngintr() at ngintr+0x190
swi_net() at swi_net+0x148
ithread_loop() at ithread_loop+0x1c4
fork_exit() at fork_exit+0x88
fork_trampoline() at fork_trampoline+0x8
db>

Have full core.

hw is SUN Netra T1.

Fix: 

none
How-To-Repeat: Install 7.0-BETA4 from src to clean disk (never tried to reproduce this from binary-install, but I don't think that something would change). Install mpd from ports, configure it to use pptp interface with default route to it, connect mpd to pptp server (i'm using 6.2STABLE i386 with mpd also).

The same configuration worked on 6.2-STABLE on the same HW without problems.
